hook: ChatGPT gave me a 5-step framework when I just needed someone to think out loud with me.
caption:
ChatGPT gave me a 5-step framework when I just needed someone to think out loud with me.

This is the thing nobody talks about.

AI defaults to *output mode* — bullet points, action items, structured plans. Because that's what most people reward it for.

But some people don't think in frameworks. They think in spirals. They need to talk around a problem three times before the real question even surfaces.

If that's you, you've probably felt this: you dump a messy problem into ChatGPT and get back something that looks helpful but lands completely wrong. Too clean. Too resolved. Like it skipped the part where you actually figure things out.

That's not an AI problem. That's a calibration problem.

When AI knows you're a spiral thinker, it stops handing you conclusions and starts asking the question underneath your question.

It holds space for the mess instead of tidying it up before you're ready.

Your thinking style isn't a bug to work around. It's the input AI is missing.

BootFile captures it. So every response actually fits how your brain works — not how the average user's brain works.

The framework people and the spiral people deserve different AI. 👇 Which one are you?
